description: Native app surfaces phase A6 (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): the multi-device ('two-phone conversation') pattern. Two startSurface steps each provision their own managed emulator (distinct device names, serial boots per A6 — the parallel array form arrives with multi-surface Phase 6), steps interleave across the devices by surface name, and the run-end sweep shuts down both emulators Doc Detective booted. Gated behind DD_FIXTURE_MULTIDEVICE (the DD_FIXTURE_PROVISION precedent) because two emulator boots are too heavy for every leg — the managed-boot KVM job sets it; everywhere else this spec lands SKIPPED via the requires gate.

tests.description: Boot two managed devices (dd-phone-a / dd-phone-b), open Settings on each, and interleave surface-named steps across them: screenshot phone A, press Back on phone A (a device key, no element criteria), screenshot phone B. Each surface-named step foregrounds its own device, so the per-device screenshots prove registry identity (device name), console-port allocation (5554/5556), per-device driver sessions, and activate-on-switch — the same way the single-device android-multi-app spec proves switching, without depending on a specific Settings row being present. Settings stands in for the chat app because it exists on every image.
